name: Sugar Snap
image_alt: Plump round snap pea pods on a tall trellis
description: The snap pea that changed how Americans eat peas — plump, crunchy pods with rounded peas inside, entirely edible pod and all. Exceptionally sweet and best eaten raw straight from the vine.
history: Developed by Dr. Calvin Lamborn at the Gallatin Valley Seed Company and introduced in 1979 as an AAS winner. It was a genetic breakthrough — combining the edible pod of a snow pea with the plump sweetness of a shelling pea.

good.reason: Quick companion that matures before peas crowd

seasonal_quirks: Vines reach 5–6 feet and need a substantial trellis. Extremely heavy producer over a 3–4 week period. Flavor peaks when cool — heat ends the season abruptly.
harvest_cues: Pods should be plump and round, skin still smooth and taut. The pod should snap cleanly. Slightly past prime pods become tough-stringed — remove the string if harvesting late.
variety_notes: The sweetness diminishes rapidly after harvest — the sugar-to-starch conversion is faster than in shelling peas. Eat within hours for peak quality, or refrigerate immediately.
preservation: Blanch and freeze — texture softens but flavor holds well.
